0.q: PpSnRK1α enhanced reactive oxygen species (ROS) metabolism by increasing the expression level of antioxidase genes and antioxidant enzyme activities

1.q: Overexpression of PpAOS3 increased the root length and jasmonic acid (JA) content and attenuated growth inhibition under salt stress in Arabidopsis.

2.q: NBT and Evans staining showed that Arabidopsis overexpressing PpAOS3 reduces O2− accumulation and cell death under salt stress.

3.q: using yeast two-hybrid screening, we isolated a nucleus-localized ZF-HD_dimer domain protein PpZFHD1, which interacts with the PpOFP1 protein in the peach cultivar 'Zhongnongpan No.10'.

6.q: PpTCP1, PpTCP13, and PpTCP15 were found to activate the expression of PpTPS1 by yeast one-hybrid (Y1H) and dual-luciferase assays.

7.q: PpTCP1, PpTCP13, and PpTCP15 were found to activate the expression of PpTPS1 by yeast one-hybrid (Y1H) and dual-luciferase assays.

8.q: PpTCP1, PpTCP13, and PpTCP15 were found to activate the expression of PpTPS1 by yeast one-hybrid (Y1H) and dual-luciferase assays.

9.q: We verified that PpTPS1 interacts with PpABI5 (confirmed by Y2H, pull-down, and BiFC assays).

10.q: The high-affinity K+ transporter 1 (HKT1) is known to play a role in the removal of Na+ from the xylem and bring it back to the root.
